message:
feat(bubbletea): add non-consuming try_downcast method to Message

The existing downcast() method consumes the Message even when the type
doesn't match, forcing callers to clone before attempting multiple
downcast targets in sequence. The new try_downcast() returns
Result<M, Message>, giving back the original Message on type mismatch
so callers can chain attempts without allocation overhead.

This enables patterns like:
  let msg = match msg.try_downcast::<FooMsg>() {
      Ok(foo) => return handle_foo(foo),
      Err(original) => original,
  };
  // try next type with `msg` still intact

Also updated the doc comment on downcast() to note the consuming
behavior and point users to try_downcast as an alternative.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
